title: Marty Supreme Costume Design by Miyako Bellizzi: Oscars 2026
text: Costume designer Miyako Bellizzi earned her first Academy Award nomination for her work on Josh Safdie's Marty Supreme, a film set in 1950s New York following a protagonist's ambitious pursuit of success. Bellizzi's team created thousands of costumes from scratch due to the extensive action sequences requiring multiple duplicates. The designer drew from historical New York documentation, including Ken Jacobs' 1955 Lower East Side footage and photography by Ruth Orkin and Robert Frank, to establish authenticity. Bellizzi dressed lead actor Timothée Chalamet in oversized, double-breasted suits with high-waisted single-pleat trousers styling that reflected his character's aspirations rather than current wealth. She noted that forties collars are kind of like seventies collars they're really big and pointy, incorporating subtle striped patterns throughout his shirt selection. For ping-pong scenes, she created casual athletic wear featuring sweatshirts over white tank tops, finding the tank top silhouette particularly challenging to authentically recreate. Gwyneth Paltrow's character transforms visually throughout the narrative. Initially presented in grayscale black-and-white tones influenced by Marlene Dietrich and Grace Kelly's aesthetics, she transitions to vibrant color particularly a red dress symbolizing emotional awakening reflecting her character's personal liberation. Bellizzi designed distinct costumes for ping-pong players representing 16 countries, tailoring each nation's aesthetic from European to East Asian to South American styles. She incorporated personal family photographs from her Japanese heritage when designing Tokyo sequences.
